data.title: Reaction Roundup: SA analysts, experts weigh in on September jobs report
data.description:
Wall Street's focus on Thursday was on the much-delayed September nonfarm payrolls report, which showed significantly stronger-than-expected job growth in the month. 

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said the economy added 119K jobs, much higher than the expected figure of 50K. However, nonfarm payroll figures for July and August saw major revisions. Moreover, the unemployment rate ticked up to 4.4% in September, the highest since October 2021.

The data complicates matters for watchers of monetary policy and the Federal Reserve. On the one hand, strong headline job growth indicates strength in the labor market. On the other, the big revisions and the tick up in the unemployment rate suggest underlying cracks.     

The strong September jobs report initially helped in spurring Wall Street to a massive open in the morning. Sentiment has soured since, and markets are now deep in the red. Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

See below for various reactions to the jobs data:

Mike Zaccardi, Seeking Alpha analyst since 2022: 

"It was a mixed, 'stale' September jobs report. The headline payrolls gain was the best since April, but the unemployment rate rose 0.12 percentage points to 4.44%, the highest this cycle.

Treasury yields fell, suggesting a greater chance of a Fed rate cut in December. The odds are only about 1-in-3, though. Unfortunately, we won't get the November employment report until December 16 (after the Dec. 9–10 Fed meeting). So I'll continue to watch alternative, private-sector-led jobs releases, along with any additional corporate layoff announcements.

In terms of price action, together with the Nvidia earnings, today likely marked a bit of a clearing event, potentially setting the stage for a classic Thanksgiving week rally and maybe a year-end melt-up."

Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital:

"It's a clear case of a Fed that will be forced to cut due to the K-Shaped economy. On one hand, we have hyperscalers that lead the market and do not require lower rates. On the other hand, we have rising unemployment and sluggish cyclical growth, forcing the Fed to ease into a strong market.

This will likely be inflationary and improve liquidity in 2026."

Chris Lau, investing group leader of DIY Value Investing:

"September’s dramatic reversal of a 119K employment increase, up from a 4K dip in August, requires scrutiny. This is in contrast to both the Challenger and ADP reports.

The BLS revised July NFP down by 7,000 and August down by 26,000. Without October’s NFP report, investors will not get a possible downward revision for September’s data. This sets up the S&P 500 (IVV) to near 7,000, up from its current 6,750 level."

Olu Sonola, head of U.S. economic research at Fitch Ratings:

"This is the quintessential 'good news is bad news' story. The upside surprise in this report is positive, but it likely dampens prospects for a rate cut in December. As the only official jobs report the Fed will see before its next interest rate decision, it reinforces the idea of proceeding cautiously when visibility is limited.

The next inflation data arrives on the morning of the December meeting, and that release could prove more consequential than usual. The slight uptick in the unemployment rate complicates the narrative—pick your poison: stronger job growth or rising unemployment, because the good news may not be as good after all."

Elizabeth Renter, senior economist at NerdWallet:

"The Fed’s decision when they meet in December is a tricky one, but there’s a good chance that would be the case even with all of the data in-hand. With risks to both sides of their dual mandate at odds, they’re seeking clarity in an incomplete picture.

We don’t yet know when the next batch of consumer inflation data will be released, and this will be the last jobs report before their meeting. Fortunately, they will get some insight into the actual state-of-play in layoffs when the October JOLTS report is released the first day they convene."

Heather Long, chief economist at Navy Federal Credit Union: 

"While it’s encouraging that 119,000 jobs were added in September, the reality is the economy lost jobs in both June and August. There are very few industries that are hiring right now outside of healthcare and restaurants.

Overall, the job picture was even worse than many realized this summer, and it didn’t improve much in September. So far, firing has not picked up. Initial jobless claims remain low. But looking across the past three months, there are numerous industries that are now shedding jobs: mining, manufacturing, transportation and warehousing, information, finance, professional and business services. There needs to be a rebound in hiring for a healthy economy.”

Mark Hamrick, senior economic analyst at Bankrate: 

"Word that payroll growth was stronger than expected in September helps to quell recession fears. Even so, there are less than comforting signs in the slight rise in the jobless rate to 4.4%, the highest in nearly four years, and in the downward revisions to payrolls for both July and August. In retrospect, August saw a slight contraction in employment.

After a forced fast of federal data caused by the government shutdown, we’re finally able to begin to feast again. A bit on the stale side, the September jobs report was collected two months ago. It’s less than fully satisfying when it comes to understanding the current economy. The November report, due in mid-December, will offer a fresher sampling of where things really stand."

Seeking Alpha editor Liz Kiesche contributed to this story

data.title: Emerson slips after introducing 2028 financial targets, outlining vision for autonomous future
data.description:
Emerson (EMR) -2.7% in Thursday's trading, turning negative along with most stocks, after introducing 2028 financial targets and a shareholder return plan while outlining its strategic priorities for "engineering the autonomous future."

Key 2028 financial targets include $21 billion in net sales via a 5% organic compound annual growth rate, a 30% adjusted segment EBITA margin, $8.00 adjusted EPS via 10% CAGR, and $12 billion cumulative free cash flow for 2026-28.

Emerson (EMR) said it plans to return $10 billion to shareholders through stock buybacks and increased dividends through 2028, and posted a through‑the‑cycle framework for 4%-7% organic sales growth, 40% incremental margins, 10% adjusted EPS growth, and 18%-20% free cash flow margin.

The company also reiterated FY 2026 guidance provided earlier this month for net sales growth of ~5.5%, underlying sales growth of ~4%, and adjusted earnings of $6.35-$6.55/share, mostly lower than the $6.54 analyst consensus estimate, suggesting modest upside after a strong FY 2025.

data.title: OpenAI's US auditor is Deloitte: report
data.description:
Amid concerns from some investors, including Michael Burry, OpenAI's (OPENAI) U.S. auditor is one of the Big 4 firms, the Financial Times reported. 

The ChatGPT maker, which has come under scrutiny in recent weeks for its intense spending plans, is audited by Deloitte, the news outlet added, citing multiple sources familiar with the matter.

OpenAI and Deloitte did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

On Wednesday, Burry, who has taken to the social media X platform to express his concerns about the artificial intelligence spending boom, openly pondered which firm was auditing OpenAI's books. “OpenAI is the linchpin here,” Burry wrote. “Can anyone name their auditor?”

Burry has also come out and said that the useful life of processors and data centers during the AI spending boom are being inflated to help company earnings.

“The idea of a useful life for depreciation being longer because chips from more than 3-4 years ago are fully booked confuses physical utilization with value creation,” Burry wrote in another post on X. “Just because something is used does not mean it is profitable. GAAP refers to economic benefits. Airlines keep old planes around for overflow during Thanksgiving or Christmas, but are only marginally profitable on the planes all the same, and not worth much at all. A100s take 2-3x more power per FLOP (compute unit) so cost 2-3x more in electricity alone than H100s. And Nvidia claims H100 is 25x less energy efficient than Blackwell for inference. If that is the direction you are going, chances are you have to be doing it, and it is not pleasant.”

Nvidia (NVDA) — which recently announced a deal to invest $100B into OpenAI — fired back at the claim that its chips from a few years ago are being used to impact the company's accounting. 

“The long useful life of NVIDIA's CUDA GPUs is a significant [total cost of ownership] advantage over accelerators,” Nvidia CFO Colette Kress said on the company's earnings call.

“Most accelerators without CUDA and NVIDIA's time-tested and versatile architecture became obsolete within a few years as model technologies evolve,” Kress added. “Thanks to CUDA, the A100 GPUs we shipped 6 years ago are still running at full utilization today, powered by [a] vastly improved software stack.”

data.title: KKR plans to raise $15B for its latest Asia fund - report
data.description:
KKR (KKR) is looking to raise $15B for its newest Asia fund as the private equity firm pitches investors on the opportunities in Japan and India, according to a media report on Thursday.

The size of the new fund would match that of the previous Asia Fund IV, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The news comes after KKR (KKR) said earlier in the month its Q4 adjusted net income per share is expected to be hampered by a one-time charge incurred by the underperformance of its second Asia PE fund.

KKR (KKR) shares slid 2.2% in midafternoon trading.

data.title: Jacobs outlines 16% adjusted EPS growth target for 2026 while expanding data center and water sector pipelines
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Jacobs Solutions Inc. (J) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Pragada emphasized strong Q4 and full-year results, stating, "We delivered strong results for Q4 and are pleased to end FY '25, the first year of our 5-year strategy on a positive note. For both the quarter and the fiscal year, we drove strong double-digit growth in adjusted EPS, supported by solid revenue growth and robust margin expansion." Pragada highlighted a 6% increase in consolidated backlog to $23.1 billion, a new company record, and noted the performance of PA Consulting with "double-digit revenue and operating profit growth in the second half of FY '25."
  • Pragada announced, "We grew Q4 adjusted EPS by 28% year-over-year, and this was primarily driven by 6% net revenue growth, a record quarterly adjusted EBITDA margin of just over 14.4% and better below-the-line performance." He stated the company finished above the high end of its adjusted EPS guidance at $6.12 for FY '25.
  • Key new project awards included the extension of an operational intelligence agreement with United Utilities in the U.K. through 2030 and a major design award for a semiconductor fabrication facility. Pragada also highlighted being selected by the MTA for the Interborough Express Light Rail project in New York.
  • CFO Venkatesh Nathamuni said, "During fiscal year '25, we delivered our commitment to drive profitable growth, which consisted of double-digit growth in both EBITDA and adjusted EPS as well as a 7% free cash flow margin." Nathamuni called attention to a "record adjusted EBITDA margin, both during the quarter and for the full fiscal year," and confirmed a record $1.1 billion in shareholder returns, with $754 million in share repurchases.

Outlook

  • Nathamuni provided guidance for fiscal year '26, projecting adjusted net revenue to increase 6% to 10% year-over-year, adjusted EBITDA margin between 14.4% and 14.7%, adjusted EPS to range from $6.90 to $7.30, and free cash flow margin in the range of 7% to 8%. He stated, "Notably, our outlook for fiscal year '26 implies 16% year-on-year growth in adjusted EPS at the midpoint."
  • Management noted the extra week in Q4 of fiscal '26 will add "just over 1.5 points to our net revenue growth rate."
  • Q1 fiscal '26 is forecasted for 5.5% to 7.5% net revenue growth and a "low to mid-15% margin," with Q1 being the seasonally slowest quarter.

Financial Results

  • For Q4, gross revenue increased 7% year-over-year, adjusted net revenue grew 6%, and adjusted EBITDA reached $324 million, up 12%. Adjusted EBITDA margin hit 14.4%, up 79 basis points. Adjusted EPS reached $1.75, a 28% increase.
  • Full-year gross revenue increased about 5% year-over-year, adjusted net revenue rose more than 5%, adjusted EBITDA was up 14%, and adjusted EPS increased 16%. Free cash flow for the year was $607 million.
  • PA Consulting revenue increased 10% in Q4 with operating profit up 17% on a 23% margin. For the full year, PA's operating growth matched Q4 performance.
  • The company ended fiscal year '25 with $1 billion in net debt and a net leverage ratio of 0.8x LTM adjusted EBITDA.

Q&A

  • Sangita Jain, KeyBanc, asked about the impact of the federal government shutdown on bookings. Pragada replied there was no impact as "those awards in the federal government happened before the shutdown."
  • Jain also inquired about PA; Pragada stated, "Our negotiations continue, and I would say they're progressing... we're on track to do so" regarding a decision by March '26.
  • Andrew Wittmann, Baird, questioned environmental sector softness and free cash flow margin guidance. Pragada explained regulatory volatility led private sector clients to pause spending, and FEMA-related public sector delays also affected results. Nathamuni clarified that guidance incorporates a one-time tax event and expected cash expenses related to PA.
  • Jamie Cook, Truist, asked about Infrastructure and Advanced Facilities margins and AI's role in future margin expansion. Nathamuni explained expected seasonal margin trends and highlighted operational improvements. Pragada detailed AI initiatives, including partnerships with NVIDIA and Palantir, and referenced a differentiated position in transport analytics.
  • Steven Fisher, UBS, sought clarification on margin expansion drivers; Nathamuni highlighted global delivery, commercial models, and AI adoption as key contributors. Pragada discussed data center pipeline growth and expanded project scope, noting a fivefold increase in pipeline.
  • Michael Dudas, Vertical Research, asked about pipeline growth and capital allocation. Pragada listed fastest-growing pipelines in data centers (up 5x), semiconductors (up 20%), life sciences (up 50%), and water (up 50%). Nathamuni affirmed a commitment to regular share repurchases.
  • Jerry Revich, Wells Fargo, inquired about achieving high-end growth targets; Nathamuni said momentum is broad-based, while Pragada emphasized Life Sciences, Data Centers, and semiconductor fabrication as key drivers for high-end performance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was positive, focusing on growth drivers, pipeline expansion, and clarification on guidance and sector-specific trends.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly highlighting record results, pipeline strength, and strategic execution. Pragada stated, "we are entering FY '26 with significant momentum."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained confident, but with even greater emphasis on future growth drivers and margin expansion, while analysts continued to probe for details on pipeline conversion and cash flow.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for adjusted EPS increased from a previous range of $6 to $6.10 to a new range of $6.90 to $7.30 for fiscal '26, with the midpoint representing 16% growth.
  • The data center pipeline saw a significant increase (5x), and semiconductor pipeline growth was highlighted as 20%.
  • Margin expansion guidance for fiscal '26 is 50 to 80 basis points, following a reported 110 basis point expansion in fiscal '25.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from backlog growth and scope expansion in Q3 to margin expansion, sector-specific performance, and capital allocation for Q4.
  • Management’s confidence in margin drivers and pipeline conversion increased, with more specificity on AI, global delivery, and sector performance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory volatility in the environmental sector caused private sector spending pauses.
  • Public sector delays were attributed to disaster relief funding and state budget reorganizations.
  • Guidance incorporates a one-time tax event and potential cash expenses tied to the PA Consulting transaction.
  • Management highlighted seasonality as a factor for Q1 fiscal '26 margin and revenue performance.

Final Takeaway

Jacobs Solutions concluded fiscal year 2025 with record results, citing robust double-digit EPS growth, margin expansion, and record backlog. The company enters 2026 with strong sector pipelines—especially in data centers, semiconductors, life sciences, and water—and expects adjusted EPS growth of 16% at the midpoint of guidance. Management reiterated confidence in its differentiated digital and AI-driven business model, continued capital returns, and pipeline conversion as key drivers for the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BrightView outlines $2.67B–$2.73B revenue target for 2026 as fleet investments and sales force expansion drive growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BrightView Holdings, Inc. (BV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Dale Asplund, President and CEO, stated that 2025 was “another transformational year” for BrightView, highlighting investments in frontline employees, service consistency, and a refreshed fleet. He noted, “We have also made progress in expanding our sales force, hiring about 100 new sellers in the year, which positions us to drive top line profitable growth in the near term.”

  • Asplund announced an increase in the share repurchase authorization from $100 million to $150 million and expressed confidence in the company’s valuation, describing it as “dislocated from the tremendous progress we have made over the past 2 years and the significant opportunities that lie ahead.” He emphasized a continued focus on “delivering sustainable and profitable top line growth in the near and long term.”

  • Asplund reported that customer retention improved by about 200 basis points from the prior year and approximately 400 basis points since October 2023, reaching around 83%.

  • Brett Urban, Executive VP and CFO, stated, “Fiscal '25 EBITDA was $352 million at a margin of 13.2%, representing a 260 basis point improvement from fiscal '23.” Urban emphasized the company’s “streamlined operating structure and unlocking scale advantages as the #1 provider in our industry.”

Outlook

  • BrightView expects 2026 revenue in the range of $2.67 billion to $2.73 billion, adjusted EBITDA between $363 million and $377 million, and adjusted free cash flow from $100 million to $115 million.

  • For Maintenance Land, revenue is projected to increase by 1% to 2%, with Development revenue expected to be flat to up 2%. Snow revenue is anticipated between $190 million and $220 million.

  • Urban said, “We expect margins in the Maintenance segment to expand by 50 to 70 basis points and margins in the Development segment to expand by 20 to 40 basis points. In total, we expect adjusted EBITDA margins to increase by 40 to 60 basis points.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, guidance now reflects a return to revenue growth in land maintenance, continued margin expansion, and a larger capital allocation to share repurchases.

Financial Results

  • Urban reported record adjusted EBITDA of $352 million for fiscal 2025, an increase of $28 million or 8% over 2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin reached 13.2%, expanding by 150 basis points year-over-year.

  • The company reduced SG&A expense as a percentage of revenue by 180 basis points since 2023 and maintained net leverage at 2.3x.

  • BrightView invested over $300 million in capital for fleet refreshment over two years, bringing the average age of core production vehicles to five years and core mowers to one year.

  • The company increased its share repurchase authorization and noted that “profits and margins we've generated since 2023 have been exceptional.”

Q&A

  • Benjamin Luke McFadden, William Blair, asked about discretionary areas of land maintenance and near-term setup. Asplund responded, “We saw optimism that discretionary spend of ancillary, we could definitely see return...the progress we saw right through Q4 is an indication of why we said we're going to grow this business as we go through 2026.”

  • McFadden followed up on new sales hires’ productivity ramp. Asplund detailed, “Usually the first 6 months of new sellers, they're learning...once they get over a year, we see them get closer to what our seasoned sales reps would sell.” Urban added that “the transformation of this business under Dale's leadership...has been nothing short of exceptional.”

  • Bob Labick, CJS Securities, asked about employee retention progress. Asplund stated, “We have improved that statistic over 3,000 basis points since the end of 2022...I think...there's another, call it, 2,000 to 3,000 basis points that we can get to get to a more normalized level.”

  • Labick also inquired about fleet investment timelines. Urban replied, “We probably have 1 more year to go to continue to refresh our trucks...in 2026 and even into 2027, we're going to really invest in refreshing our trailers.”

  • Andrew Wittmann, Baird, discussed operational efficiencies and technology. Asplund highlighted digitized sales training and field service management, while Urban noted ongoing efficiency gains in procurement and technology.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was positive, frequently acknowledging progress and expressing interest in further growth drivers and operational efficiency.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout, with Asplund emphasizing, “This is our time for growth” and Urban reinforcing excitement about continued investments.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to assertive confidence, reflecting improved KPIs and successful execution of strategic initiatives. Analyst sentiment also improved, with fewer questions about macro headwinds and more focus on growth levers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 now projects revenue growth in land maintenance, whereas the previous quarter reaffirmed a flat to positive outlook.

  • The share repurchase program was expanded from $100 million to $150 million.

  • Capital investment in fleet intensified, and the company reported further improvements in customer and employee retention.

  • Management tone was more assertive and forward-looking, in contrast to the prior quarter’s focus on foundational improvements and uncertainty around discretionary revenue.

  • Analyst focus shifted from macro headwinds and contract revenue concerns to sales force productivity, capital allocation, and operational efficiency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted potential noise from seasonality and weather-related disruptions in the first half of the year, with Asplund cautioning, “the next 6 months or 2 quarters, we could have some noise from the seasonality of the business.”

  • Project delays in the Development segment were acknowledged, but both Asplund and Urban expressed confidence that these would be temporary and offset by backlog and new cold starts.

  • Labor market challenges, particularly linked to immigration policy, were discussed, but management highlighted investments in employee retention as a mitigating factor.

Final Takeaway

BrightView’s management underscored that substantial investments in fleet, frontline employees, and a growing sales force have laid a strong foundation for sustainable top-line growth. With a marked improvement in customer and employee retention, ongoing operational efficiencies, and a significant increase in share repurchase authorization, the company projects record revenue and EBITDA for 2026. Management’s focus remains on unlocking further growth opportunities and delivering consistent value for shareholders through disciplined capital allocation and continued investment in core business drivers.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street turns red after the Nasdaq's morning 2% rally
data.description:
U.S. stocks gave back early gains on Thursday, erasing a morning rally that had pushed the Nasdaq Composite above 2%.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last down by 1.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) had pushed down by 0.8% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) dipped by 0.3%. 

From a sector-by-sector point of view, three of the 11 S&P segments find themselves up in positive territory and are led by Consumer Staples. Going in the other direction, Info Tech has been the weakest performer thus far.

As for stocks that were on the move, Nvidia was last -2.2% after adding more than $200B to its market cap with its post-earnings rally, nearing the $200/share level it lost early this month.

Thursday's spotlight was also on the September nonfarm payrolls report, which was initially supposed to come out on October 3. However, the longest U.S. government shutdown in history derailed that release.

The data showed the U.S. labor market added 119K jobs in September, easily outstripping the consensus of 50K. The unemployment rate ticked up to 4.4% in the month, a tad higher than the expected 4.3%.

“Thursday's jobs report was much stronger than expected and it's possible that the Federal Reserve may take more of a wait and see approach to rates in December, especially since there is still uncertainty over the economic data, which has only first started to come back online after the drought from the government shutdown. Thursday's jobs report suggests that the labor market is not as weak as feared,” Alexander Guiliano, chief investment officer, Resonate Wealth Partners stated.

Moreover, Treasury yields shifted lower as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved down by 4 basis points to 4.09% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down by 4 basis points to 3.55%.

data.title: B. Riley calls out an attractive entry point on Genius Sports for investors
data.description:
B. Riley Securities defended its bullish view on Genius Sports (GENI) on Thursday, calling the recent sector-driven weakness for shares a compelling entry point for investors due to the strong fundamentals in place.

Analyst Josh Nichols said the sports data company's strong operating performance was amplified by several sector headwinds facing its B2C customers, including structural hold deterioration and general prediction market threat concerns. "The market is incorrectly treating GENI as a transaction-based sportsbook proxy when the business is fundamentally a data and technology platform provider with contracted recurring revenue, minimal betting outcome exposure, and exclusive content relationships locked through 2029/2030," he advised.

Looking ahead, Nichols and his team are optimistic about Genius Sports (GENI) heading into the December 3 analyst day event in New York City. The expectation is that management will articulate a multi-year roadmap demonstrating how the company reaches $1 billion+ annual revenue by 2027-2028 with EBITDA margins progressing toward management's 30%+ target, reestablishing GENI's differentiation as a lower-volatility platform technology company with a significant competitivemoat. 

Importantly, B. Riley sees prediction market concerns pivoting to opportunity. "While the rapid growth of sports betting through prediction markets has been a significant headwind for gaming shares, we believe the nascent category could represent a meaningful market expansion opportunity as regulatory clarity emerges," assessed Nichols. As the category matures, the need for official league data, marks and logos, and integrity solutions is seen intensifying, which should work to Genius Sports (GENI) favor.

B. Riley has a Buy rating on Genius Sports (GENI) and a price target of $14.

Shares  of Genius Sports (GENI) were up 2.2% in Thursday afternoon action.

data.title: Drugmakers to develop pharma manufacturing training site in Virginia
data.description:
• AstraZeneca (AZN), Eli Lilly (LLY), and Merck have signed a memorandum of understanding with the Virginia Innovation Partnership Corp. to develop a pharmaceutical manufacturing training facility. 
  • The drugmakers have pledged $120M in workforce investment for the project.
  • The Virginia Center for Advanced Pharmaceutical Manufacturing will work with some of the state's top universities, including the University of Virginia, Virginia Commonwealth University, Virginia Tech, Old Dominion University, and James Madison University.
  • "The Center will prepare students and workers for the next generation of high-quality, high-demand advanced manufacturing jobs in biopharma through hands-on GMP-simulated training, industry credentials and certifications, and employer apprenticeships and internships," according to a news release.
  • Earlier this year, AstraZeneca, Eli Lilly, and Merck announced multi-billion-dollar manufacturing facility investments in Virginia.

data.title: Top retail Quant stocks as Walmart is S&P 500’s top gainer
data.description:
The retail sector is showing strong performance on Thursday, despite volatility in the markets, with Walmart (WMT) leading the gains among S&P 500 stocks with a nearly 6.4% jump on Thursday after the company rose full year sales growth outlook.

Using Seeking Alpha’s proprietary Quant Ratings system, we’ve identified the top retail stocks that demonstrate the most promising investment characteristics according to a sophisticated trading algorithm.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings have consistently outperformed the market, with their very bullish recommendations beating the S&P 500 (SP500) by 1,755% compared to 379% from January 2010 to June 2021.

The Quant Model identifies stocks that collectively hold the best investment characteristics across five key factors: growth, profitability, value, momentum, and rising analysts’ earnings estimates. This automated stock selection process efficiently sorts through vast amounts of data beyond human capacity, providing investors with a data-driven approach to stock selection.

Top retail stocks by Quant rating:

  1. Citi Trends (CTRN) - Strong Buy rating of 4.84, showing significant market resilience with year-to-date growth of 161.47%.
  2. The Children’s Place (PLCE) - Strong Buy rating of 4.73, despite current challenges, it reported 72.69% year-to-date performance.
  3. Envela Corporation (ELA) - Strong Buy rating of 4.72, with impressive operational metrics and a 13.83% increase year-to-date.
  4. Lands’ End (LE) - Strong Buy rating of 4.69, reflecting a year-to-date return of 9.76% in the apparel sector.
  5. Zumiez (ZUMZ) - Strong Buy rating of 4.68, maintaining investor confidence with a momentum of 11.31% year-to-date.
  6. Outdoor Holding Co. (POWW) - Buy rating of 4.41, despite facing a recent decline, it holds a modest year-to-date growth of 7.36%.
  7. Urban Outfitters (URBN) - Buy rating of 4.34, securing a notable year-to-date growth percentage of 16.74%.
  8. Boot Barn Holdings (BOOT) - Buy rating of 4.31, sustained a positive trajectory with an 18.97% increase year-to-date.
  9. Genesco (GCO) - Buy rating of 3.93, showcasing a growth of 11.99% year-to-date amidst competitive retail dynamics.
  10. National Vision Holdings (EYE) - Buy rating of 3.89, with growth stability represented by an 8.79% performance year-to-date.
  11. Coupang (CPNG) - Buy rating of 3.81, benefiting from a 43.60% uptick year-to-date, driven by dynamic market strategies.
  12. Stitch Fix (SFIX) - Buy rating of 3.73, adjusting strategies to achieve an 18.51% improvement year-to-date.
  13. Amazon (AMZN) - Hold rating of 3.49, recorded 23.22% year-to-date growth, reflecting its robust marketplace.
  14. The RealReal (REAL) - Hold rating of 3.48, managing to achieve a 28.75% year-to-date growth.
  15. Kohl’s Corp. (KSS) - Hold rating of 3.46, reflecting strategic adjustments with a year-to-date performance of -9.66%.
